_doc: Opposing arguments encountered in court. Schema per opponent: { id, chapter, display_name (Design), court_rounds[] — one entry per casebook_judge_state value, each with: round_tag, opening_statement (Design), moves[{ move_id, article_tags, principle_tags, context_tags, base_strength (Code), flavor_line (Design) }], defeat_lines (Design), partial_lines (Design) }. Tag values must exist in data/tag_taxonomy.json.

opponents._doc: Landlord's counsel at the District Court, Beat 12. Unnamed throughout — the judge does not address him by name (see judge_district_ch1.json _address_forms). Recurring in Ch2 as Attorney Grzyb; do not use that name here.

court_rounds._doc: Round 1 — Defective service. casebook_judge_state = 'round_1_open' (bench prompt) then 'round_1_react' (judge dry-surprise after player argues). Opponent opens before player chooses a move.
court_rounds.opening_statement: The file records service. The address used is the address on the tenancy. The court cannot reopen a hearing that was properly notified.

moves.on_intro: The opposing file insists the client was served because a box was ticked.
moves.on_defeat: The formal service argument collapses under the weight of basic procedural fairness.
moves.on_partial: The argument weakens, but the court wants a cleaner link to notice.
moves.flavor_line: The address appears in the file. That is the end of it.

moves.on_intro: Someone accepted the notice at that address. Service is not the petitioner's problem.
moves.on_defeat: Third-party acceptance cannot cure notice to a wrong address when the serving party knew of the renumbering.
moves.on_partial: The adult-occupant point stands, but the knowledge element is still in dispute.
moves.flavor_line: The current occupant of number seven received the notice and signed for it. The form is satisfied.

defeat_lines: The service question is answered. The renumbering speaks for itself.
The court has heard enough on notice.
partial_lines: The affidavit is supplementary. Knowledge alone does not settle the form.
The address is on the record. The court will hear the third clause.

court_rounds._doc: Round 2 — Fair hearing. casebook_judge_state = 'round_2_open' then 'round_2_react'. Single-branch (no stance variation — judge_district_ch1.json react_round_2 is stance-agnostic).
court_rounds.opening_statement: The procedure is a technicality. The landlord's position on the underlying tenancy is sound. Reopening the hearing merely delays an inevitable outcome.

moves.on_intro: The opposing argument treats procedure as decoration.
moves.on_defeat: The right to a fair hearing is not a technicality. It is the condition under which everything else is decided.
moves.on_partial: The court is not unsympathetic to efficiency, but the Article 6 point requires an answer.
moves.flavor_line: Procedure is only the door. What matters is what is behind it.

moves.on_intro: The underlying merits of the landlord's claim are strong enough to make the procedural point academic.
moves.on_defeat: The merits are not before the court at this hearing. They cannot pre-empt a fair-hearing objection.
moves.on_partial: The argument has some force, but the procedural defect must be addressed first.
moves.flavor_line: Whatever the process, the conclusion would be the same. We are wasting the court's afternoon.

defeat_lines: The third-clause point lands. The court will proceed to remedy.
partial_lines: The argument weakens, but the landlord's position on the underlying tenancy is not yet resolved.
Procedure is acknowledged. The court wants a cleaner evidentiary link.

court_rounds._doc: Round 3 — Remedy. casebook_judge_state = 'round_3_open' then 'round_3_remedy'. After player argues remedy, judge delivers the remedy announcement (in judge_district_ch1.json remedy_round_3_<stance>).
court_rounds.opening_statement: If the court is inclined to grant any remedy, it should be limited to a technical notation. An adjournment would prejudice the landlord's position and delay a legitimate eviction.

moves.on_intro: The temptation to ask for total victory enters the courtroom.
moves.on_defeat: A procedural reset is the correct remedy. The merits remain open; the hearing is restored.
moves.on_partial: The remedy ask is noted, but the court will calibrate to the procedural defect only.
moves.flavor_line: Any remedy granted now should not predetermine the merits of the underlying tenancy. We ask for proportionality.

moves.on_intro: The landlord has spent resources on this matter. An adjournment imposes an unfair cost.
moves.on_defeat: The cost of rescheduling falls on the party whose defective service caused the problem.
moves.on_partial: The prejudice point requires a factual foundation the landlord has not supplied.
moves.flavor_line: An adjournment costs the respondent. That cost is real and before the court.

defeat_lines: The motion to set aside is granted. The matter is rescheduled.
The procedural reset stands. Notice will be re-served in the ordinary course.
partial_lines: The remedy is calibrated. The underlying tenancy is not before the court.
The court reserves the merits. The hearing is restored on the service point only.
